title: China-to-Peru Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: China GB/T Baseline vs Peru NTP-IEC — Standards Gap
rows.target_requirement: Peru's technical standards framework for PV inverters is IEC-based through INACAL NTP adoption. The relevant international standards are IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 for safety and IEC 61683 for performance. Peru does not recognize China's GB/T series (GB/T 37408, GB/T 19964, NB/T 32004) as equivalent for market access. Exporters holding only China-domestic GB test reports must supplement these with IEC-based third-party test evidence before seeking Peru market entry.
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter manufacturers use NB/T 32004, GB/T 37408 (anti-islanding), GB/T 19964 (large-scale PV power plants connecting to grid), and GB/T 30427 for domestic compliance. These do not serve as Peru NTP equivalents.
rows.gap: Map each China GB/T or NB/T requirement to the corresponding IEC clause. Identify any technical requirements in GB/T that differ from IEC (e.g., different protection thresholds, different anti-islanding methods). Obtain IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 third-party test reports for the exported model.

mandatory_status.target: IEC-based test evidence is the mandatory conformity baseline for Peru market access under INACAL NTP; GB/T reports alone are not accepted
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for China domestic market under NB/T 32004; IEC test reports are voluntarily held for export

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] China GB/T and NB/T test reports do not satisfy Peru's IEC-based NTP requirements. Obtain IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 third-party test reports for the Peru export model and confirm the NTP acceptance route with INACAL.

rows.requirement: Peru 60 Hz Grid Settings and NTCSE Compliance
rows.target_requirement: Peru operates a 60 Hz electricity grid. The Norma Técnica de Calidad de los Servicios Eléctricos (NTCSE), approved by DS 020-97-EM and supervised by Osinergmin, sets quality-of-service requirements including voltage and frequency tolerances. PV inverter firmware, protection settings, anti-islanding parameters, reconnection timing, power-factor capability, and nameplate values must match Peru's 60 Hz, 220 V single-phase / 380 V three-phase grid conditions. COES (Comité de Operación Económica del Sistema Interconectado Nacional — COES/SEIN) technical requirements apply to grid-connected generation. Inverter files should confirm the Peru firmware profile, trip settings, and reconnection windows are distinct from China's 50 Hz factory default.
target_standards: NTCSE — Norma Técnica de Calidad de los Servicios Eléctricos (DS 020-97-EM, Osinergmin)
Peru 60 Hz, 220 V / 380 V grid nominal conditions
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese domestic grid-connected PV inverter settings are configured for 50 Hz operation under GB/T 19964, GB/T 37408, and NB/T 32004. A dual-frequency hardware declaration is not sufficient; the shipped unit must carry verified 60 Hz firmware, protection parameters, and site commissioning records for Peru.
rows.gap: The key gap is settings control. Exporters must not ship a China 50 Hz default profile and leave the project to adjust undocumented parameters on site. Provide a Peru 60 Hz firmware release note, protection-setting matrix, and commissioning lock procedure before shipment.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid connection because installed settings must match NTCSE and the COES/SEIN interconnection approval for the project
mandatory_status.cn: China domestic settings are 50 Hz and do not reflect Peru's 60 Hz grid conditions

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Confirm and lock the Peru 60 Hz firmware profile before shipment or commissioning. A China 50 Hz type test does not prove Peru grid-setting compliance. Verify protection parameters against NTCSE and the project interconnection approval.

source.publisher: Organismo Supervisor de la Inversión en Energía y Minería (Osinergmin)

rows.requirement: MINEM / Osinergmin RER Renewable Energy Concession and Grid Approval
rows.target_requirement: Solar PV projects in Peru connected to the national grid (SEIN) require a concession or authorization from MINEM (Ministry of Energy and Mines) under the RER renewable energy framework (Decreto Legislativo 1002 and its regulations) and technical approval from COES/SEIN for the interconnection. Osinergmin supervises compliance of generation units with Peruvian grid-quality norms. The inverter technical file submitted to COES/SEIN or the project developer should reflect Peru-specific 60 Hz settings, protection relay coordination, anti-islanding behavior, reactive power capability, and any additional requirements set by the distribution or transmission company receiving the generation.
target_standards: Decreto Legislativo 1002 (Ley de Promoción de la Inversión para la Generación de Electricidad con el Uso de Energías Renovables)
rows.china_equivalent: China domestic grid-tied PV projects are approved under the National Energy Administration (NEA) and utility grid-connection rules. These Chinese approval documents are not recognized by MINEM, Osinergmin, or COES/SEIN. The exported inverter technical file must be adapted for Peru's regulatory requirements.
rows.gap: Prepare an inverter technical specification package in Spanish that covers Peru 60 Hz grid settings, reactive power and voltage regulation capability, protection relay coordination, anti-islanding compliance, and any COES/SEIN data-communications requirements. This package supports the project's MINEM and COES/SEIN approval process.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for utility-scale grid-connected projects; RER concession from MINEM and COES/SEIN interconnection approval are project prerequisites
mandatory_status.cn: China domestic grid approval documents are not recognized by Peru's regulatory bodies

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Grid-connected PV projects in Peru require MINEM authorization and COES/SEIN technical approval. Prepare a Peru-specific inverter technical file in Spanish reflecting 60 Hz grid parameters and local protection requirements.

source.publisher: Organismo Supervisor de la Inversión en Energía y Minería (Osinergmin), Plataforma del Estado Peruano

rows.target_requirement: Peru's INACAL adopted NTP-IEC 62109-1:2024 and NTP-IEC 62109-2:2024 (safety for power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems) via Directoral Resolution No. 035-2024-INACAL/DN. These Peruvian Technical Standards align with IEC 62109 Part 1 (general requirements) and Part 2 (requirements for inverters). Products placed on the Peruvian market that fall within this scope should demonstrate conformity with these NTP editions. Third-party test reports based on IEC 62109 from accredited bodies are the common evidence route; confirmation of the specific NTP version and local acceptance route should be verified with INACAL or Peruvian customs brokers before shipment.
rows.china_equivalent: China commonly relies on NB/T 32004 (technical requirements for photovoltaic grid-connected inverters) and related standards for domestic market PV inverters. IEC 62109-based test reports are also obtained by Chinese manufacturers for export markets. NB/T 32004 and domestic safety test reports do not substitute for NTP-IEC 62109 conformity confirmation in Peru.
china_standards: IEC 62109 (export test reports from Chinese manufacturers)
rows.gap: Confirm that the exported inverter model has a valid IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 test report from an accredited laboratory accepted in Peru. Verify the NTP edition and local acceptance mechanism with INACAL or a Peruvian customs or technical specialist before shipment. A NB/T 32004 report alone is not a substitute.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where NTP-IEC 62109 applies to the product; voluntary compliance basis prior to mandatory enforcement if Peru's market surveillance has not yet been activated for this scope — verify current status with INACAL
mandatory_status.cn: NB/T 32004 is a mandatory industry standard for domestic Chinese market; IEC 62109 test reports are voluntarily obtained for export

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Obtain IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 test reports from an accredited laboratory before exporting PV inverters to Peru. Confirm the NTP-IEC 62109 edition and local acceptance route with INACAL or a Peruvian specialist.

source.publisher: TÜV Rheinland regulatory tracker (citing INACAL Resolución Directoral No. 035-2024-INACAL/DN)

rows.requirement: EMC and Electrical Safety Evidence for Peru Market
rows.target_requirement: Beyond inverter-specific safety, PV inverters imported into Peru should carry IEC-based electrical safety and EMC test evidence relevant to the product category. Peru does not operate a CE-type mandatory marking system for these products, but INACAL NTP adoption of IEC safety standards means test evidence from IEC 62109, IEC 61000-series EMC, and applicable IEC protection-class standards is the practical baseline for customs and buyer due diligence. Peruvian customs may require technical documentation at importation; the specific documentation set should be confirmed with an import specialist.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1, IEC 62109-2 (adopted as NTP in Peru)
IEC 61000 series EMC requirements as referenced in applicable NTP
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese inverter manufacturers commonly hold IEC 62109 and CE-based EMC test reports for export models. These export reports are more relevant to Peru than domestic GB standards, but the specific Peru NTP acceptance route and customs documentation requirements should be confirmed locally.
rows.gap: Prepare IEC-based safety and EMC test evidence for the exported inverter model and confirm the import documentation set with a Peruvian customs specialist before shipment.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where NTP safety standards require conformity evidence; customs documentation requirements are mandatory at import
mandatory_status.cn: China domestic GB EMC evidence does not satisfy Peru NTP-IEC or customs requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Prepare IEC-based electrical safety and EMC test documentation for export inverters. Confirm Peru import documentation and NTP acceptance requirements with local specialists before shipment.

source.publisher: Instituto Nacional de Calidad (INACAL), Plataforma del Estado Peruano
